method: #227-style, two rounds: 3 repos x top-10 default families; judge agent follows docs/agent-recipe.md with family JSON only (no source); span-matched to v5 labels. Round 1 = initial recipe; round 2 = after adding the two step-6 calibration bullets the round-1 errors indicated.

rows.note: 20 token-identical 4-line fn main copies (mean_score 1.0, value_jaccard 1.0, params 0) across tests/derive_ui fixtures; per calibration, fixture location never excuses duplication.

7 per-shell Generator::generate wrappers with identical bodies (value and shape jaccard 1.0, params 0) — near-identical per-variant siblings are the textbook default-trait-method hoist, not parallel-by-design.
7 flatten_* tests share 26 of 33 lines with only 2 varying spots (test name plus the builder setting under test), shape_jaccard 0.924 — a cheap table/parameter extraction.
11 default_values_if tests share 16 of 18 lines with 2 spots (name plus try_get_matches_from input vector) — input/expected pairs over one shared skeleton.

5 copies of the 6-line idiomatic Display-via-ValueEnum fmt impl spread over three crates plus examples; Rust trait coherence forces a per-type impl, so the abstraction saves almost nothing (dup_lines 24).
mean_value_jaccard 0.262 vs shape 0.934 (surface likeness, skeptical per step 3): each arg! macro-grammar test asserts different expected semantics behind the shared skeleton.
copy-paste-run witness over an 85-line block (shared_lines 59) duplicated between clap_complete and clap_complete_nushell test support files — classic copy-paste; a shared test util is the refactor.
5 req_delimiter tests share 23 of 35 lines with 2 spots (name plus the argv literal under test) — same assertion skeleton parameterized by input form.
5 identical 12-token fn main copies (value_jaccard 1.0, params 0) in examples/tutorial_derive; per calibration, example location does not excuse identical handlers.
copy-paste-run witness: 4 token-identical 10-line blocks inside one test file (params 0) — direct copy-paste a small test helper removes.

8 per-language printer _print_Assignment/_print_Relational methods with value_jaccard 0.92 / shape 0.979 — near-identical per-backend siblings belong on the shared CodePrinter base, not parallel-by-design.
19 two-line __hash__ = super().__hash__() dunders (shared_lines 2); Python resets __hash__ whenever __eq__ is defined, so this boilerplate must be restated per class and no abstraction can absorb it.
copy-paste-run witness on a 79-line token-identical block (params 0) duplicated between test_matrices.py and test_reductions.py — pure redundant copy.
copy-paste-run witness on a 179-line token-identical block (params 0) duplicated between test_matrixbase.py and test_matrices.py — the largest pure copy in the set.
16 identical 2-line _eval_conjugate methods (value and shape jaccard 1.0) concentrated in hyperbolic/trigonometric siblings — identical per-variant bodies hoist into the shared function base class.
the same test_issue_4564 body (11 of 12 lines shared, score 0.832, shared-sub-dag witness) is copied into three different test files — a shared regression test/helper removes it.

2 copies of test_diag_make sharing 69 of 77 lines where both varying spots are only the matrix class name (SpecialOnlyMatrix vs Matrix) — class-name-only spots are the textbook parameterize case per calibration.
6 identical 5-line _construct_body classmethods (value and shape jaccard 1.0, params 0) across codegen ast/cnodes/fnodes — one shared constructor function serves all Token classes.
copy-paste-run witness across 4 stats test files sharing 11 of 13 lines with a single spot (the sampled distribution) — a sample-consistency assertion helper absorbs it.
exact-value-graph but only 6 value nodes: a 2-line _eval_is_zero with dup_lines 12 scattered over 3 unrelated modules — the smallest family here; a cross-module mixin would cost more than it saves.
exact-value-graph proof for 43 identical 5-line exceptionCaught handlers, almost all under example/ — the calibration's literal case that example location never excuses a worthy extract.

shared_lines 0 with mean_value_jaccard 0.448 / shape 0.552 over 79 heterogeneous members (assorted constructors, a class, even a C function across 2 languages) — fuzzy shape likeness with nothing extractable.
9 identical 8-line toString methods (value and shape jaccard 1.0, params 0) across sibling protocol-constant classes (DnsOpCode, Socks4/5 types) — identical bodies hoist into a shared constant base.

copy-paste-run over 26 test classes sharing 15 lines where the single varying spot is the class-declaration line (name plus superclass) — name-only variation is textbook extract-base, not parallel-by-design.
20 identical 6-line configure overrides (value and shape jaccard 1.0, params 0) across the IoUringBufferRing test classes — one shared buffer-ring configuration helper removes all of them.

whole example server classes match only fuzzily (value_jaccard 0.445, shape 0.549, shared 13 of 29 lines): each demo genuinely encodes a different protocol pipeline behind the common bootstrap skeleton.
6 identical 13-line shutdownOutputDone methods (value and shape jaccard 1.0) across epoll/kqueue/io_uring/nio/oio channel variants — identical per-transport bodies are the textbook base-class hoist.

33 identical setMaxMessagesPerRead overrides whose only varying spot is the covariant return type (value and shape jaccard 1.0) — the calibration's named covariant-return case, extract-base not parallel-by-design.

31 identical setWriteBufferHighWaterMark overrides differing only in the declared config class in the signature spot (jaccard 1.0, shared_lines 5) — covariant-return boilerplate for a self-typed base.
31 identical setWriteBufferLowWaterMark overrides with a single class-name spot (value and shape jaccard 1.0) — same covariant-return family; textbook extract-base per calibration.
